title: Mechanical Design Engineer
description:
Xylem is a Fortune 500 global water solutions company dedicated to advancing sustainable impact and empowering the people who make water work every day. As a leading water technology company with 23,000 employees operating in over 150 countries, Xylem is at the forefront of addressing the world's most critical water challenges. We invite passionate individuals to join our team, dedicated to exceeding customer expectations through innovative and sustainable solutions.

Job Summary:
Xylem GCC Vadodara / Chennai campus is searching for a Mechanical Design Engineer to support and enhance Xylem’s mechanical product portfolio for global water Treatment Product portfolio. The role primarily focuses on sustaining engineering, value engineering/VA-VE, and new product development (NPD) to ensure design robustness, manufacturability, cost-optimization, and product competitiveness. Engineer will lead complex design enhancements, manage engineering change processes, drive problem-solving initiatives, collaborate with global stakeholders, and promote continuous improvement. This position is ideal for a proactive, detail-oriented engineer passionate about product performance and innovation.

Essential Duties/Principal Responsibilities: 

Lead sustaining engineering tasks including engineering change requests, drawing updates, and processing documentation.
Drive VA/VE initiatives to achieve cost savings, material optimization, and performance improvements.
Support and contribute to New Product Development (NPD) and technology upgrade projects — early concept design through manufacturing release.
Develop detailed 3D/2D CAD models, GD&T drawings, tolerance stack-ups, BoMs, and related manufacturing documentation.
Conduct DFMEA, DFM, DFA, prototype validation support, and technical reviews.
Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams including Manufacturing, Supply Chain, Quality, Procurement and Global Design Centers.
Ensure adherence to global engineering standards, compliance, and configuration management practices.

Must Have:

Expertise in any CAD Software (solid modelling, detailing, surfacing, GD&T). (Preferred- Autodesk Inventor)
Know-how of manufacturing processes — casting, machining, injection molding, surface coating
Ability to independently manage design tasks with accountability and milestone ownership.

Good to Have:   

Understanding of industrial products & various manufacturing processes.
Knowledge of GD&T, DFM & DFMEA and familiarity with Autodesk Vault.
Influential interpersonal skills & continues learning mindset.
Adaptability to work for cross-functional/culture and time zones.
Strong communication and collaboration skills across teams and regions.

Qualifications: 

4-Year bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering from a recognized university.
0 - 3 years’ relevant experience in product design, sustaining engineering, and VA/VE projects.

Join the global Xylem team to be a part of innovative technology solutions transforming water usage, conservation, and re-use. Our products impact public utilities, industrial sectors, residential areas, and commercial buildings, with a commitment to providing smart metering, network technologies, and advanced analytics for water, electric, and gas utilities. Partner with us in creating a world where water challenges are met with ingenuity and dedication; where we recognize the power of inclusion and belonging in driving innovation and allowing us to compete more effectively around the world.

title: Data Modeling Specialist
description:
Xylem is a Fortune 500 global water solutions company dedicated to advancing sustainable impact and empowering the people who make water work every day. As a leading water technology company with 23,000 employees operating in over 150 countries, Xylem is at the forefront of addressing the world's most critical water challenges. We invite passionate individuals to join our team, dedicated to exceeding customer expectations through innovative and sustainable solutions.

Role Overview

We are looking for a CPQ Data Modeling & Automation Specialist to join our dynamic team responsible for modeling Xylem products in Tacton CPQ. This role blends engineering knowledge, data modeling expertise, and software automation skills to ensure accurate, scalable, and efficient product configurations.

You’ll work closely with technical documentation, engineering teams, and business stakeholders to structure product data, implement configuration logic, and support automation initiatives that improve data quality and reduce manual effort.

Key Responsibilities

Product Modeling & Configuration

- Gather product requirements from engineering documents, brochures, and engineering documents.

- Structure and model product data in Tacton CPQ using constraints, rules, and dependencies.

- Design user-friendly configuration interfaces and implement smart part number logic.

Automation & Quality Assurance

- Develop scripts/tools (e.g., Python, VBA) to automate data validation and error detection.

- Build workflows to streamline data loading and testing processes.

- Support internal and external flow testing and QA automation.

Data Integration & Management

- Collect and organize material, motor, and dimensional data into the product database, and ensure it is properly integrated with the product configuration system. The data should dynamically respond to user selections during the configuration process, displaying only relevant options based on chosen parameters.

- Collaborate with pricing teams to integrate IPL tables and dynamic pricing modules.

Support & Collaboration

- Work cross-functionally with Engineering, Marketing, and Product Management.

- Collaborate with integration teams and support API-based communication between Tacton CPQ and custom web applications. Assist in debugging and resolving integration-related issues from a modeling perspective.

Qualifications

- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or Mechanical Engineering with strong software skills.

- 3+ years of experience in CPQ modeling, preferably with Tacton CPQ.

- Proficiency in Excel, Python, VBA, or similar scripting tools.

- Experience in data validation, automation, and quality control.

- Understanding of API integration concepts, especially in relation to Tacton CPQ and custom web applications. Ability to debug and support integration-related issues.

- Strong analytical skills and attention to detail.

- Familiarity with Agile methodologies, JIRA, and PLM/ERP systems is a plus.

Join the global Xylem team to be a part of innovative technology solutions transforming water usage, conservation, and re-use. Our products impact public utilities, industrial sectors, residential areas, and commercial buildings, with a commitment to providing smart metering, network technologies, and advanced analytics for water, electric, and gas utilities. Partner with us in creating a world where water challenges are met with ingenuity and dedication; where we recognize the power of inclusion and belonging in driving innovation and allowing us to compete more effectively around the world.

title: EMBEDDED ELECTRONICS HW FW ENGINEER
description:
Xylem is a Fortune 500 global water solutions company dedicated to advancing sustainable impact and empowering the people who make water work every day. As a leading water technology company with 23,000 employees operating in over 150 countries, Xylem is at the forefront of addressing the world's most critical water challenges. We invite passionate individuals to join our team, dedicated to exceeding customer expectations through innovative and sustainable solutions.

Essential Duties/Principal Responsibilities: 

Perform technical feasibility study and firmware architecture design and integration
Model, simulate, analyze the sub systems to evaluate product and design concepts
Design and develop Micro-Controller / DSP based control & Communication boards for Variable Frequency Drives, Water Sensing and IoT kind of applications.
Design and validation of critical firmware components and integrate for the product / application.
Design and develop Firmware drivers and Communication Algorithms in C, C++
Participate in FMEA and risk assessment activities
Follows standard R&D software guidelines for creating code, documenting code, storing code, testing and implementing the software, in the field when necessary.
Interface as needed with product management, customers, marketing & sales in providing technical guidance on products developed.
Be proactive in understanding product/service roadmaps and the implications of new features with peer engineers or self-training.
Other responsibilities as assigned.

Minimum Qualifications: Education, Experience, Skills, Abilities, License/Certification:

4-Year bachelor’s degree / 4 + 2-Year Master’s degree in Electronics & Communication
2-4 Years of Embedded Firmware designs
Use of recognized best practice firmware/software development methodologies and processes
Expert in Embedded Firmware designs on various controller platforms like ST / TI / Espressif / Telit and knowledge in analog and motor control hardware designs.
Expert in Communication Firmware designs based on various physical layers – Wired and Wireless like RS232 / RS485 / CAN / Bluetooth / Wi-Fi / Bluetooth (BLE) / NFC.
Expert in implementing the firmware designs for Communication Protocols like MODBUS, MQTT, CAN, SPI, I2C and Custom Protocols for board to board communication in multi controller systems.
Knowledge of Cloud Computing, Database Management, Mobile App development concepts would be useful.
Experience in FW designs with C/C++ language and on Real Time Operating Systems (RTOS) 
Expert in using the development platform like Keil / IAR / MPLAB
Expert in using debugging tools, Code Analysis Tools
Familiarity with Instrumentation and measurement of power converters
Knowledge of requirement management and Application life cycle tools.  
Able to work in multi-disciplinary and remote-located teams.
Experience in motor control software and VFD development would be preferable
Familiar with UL, CSA, FM, FCC standards and regulatory requirements
Previous experience in electromechanical industry would be preferred

Additional Information:  
Working Conditions

Electrical test labs, high voltage potentials.
Pump controls & test machines and rooms, high voltage potentials, mechanical hazards (moving machinery), high temperature hazards (hot metal,  sparks)
Occasional travel, both domestic and international

Stamina and Adaptability

Capable of working with cross functional team
Adaptability to work with different work culture, time zones.
Extensive lab testing, moving small equipment, etc.

Join the global Xylem team to be a part of innovative technology solutions transforming water usage, conservation, and re-use. Our products impact public utilities, industrial sectors, residential areas, and commercial buildings, with a commitment to providing smart metering, network technologies, and advanced analytics for water, electric, and gas utilities. Partner with us in creating a world where water challenges are met with ingenuity and dedication; where we recognize the power of inclusion and belonging in driving innovation and allowing us to compete more effectively around the world.
